summary: I moved to the United Arab Emirates when I was nineteen and lived there with my husband for twenty years. In 2009, the financial crisis hit the U.A.E., and my husband lost his job. Because of that, we had to leave the country and start a new chapter elsewhere.

After leaving the U.A.E., my children and I moved to New Delhi, India, with our eight children. We stayed there for five years, during which life was very challenging but also full of learning for my children. New Delhi became a place where we faced cultural differences and new schools, shaping our understanding of change.

India was our home for five years after we left the U.A.E. My children and I had student visas, but my husband had to return to Somalia. Life in India was difficult compared to before, but we received a lot of help from the United Nations, which supported us during that time.

My husband returned to Somalia after we moved to India because he couldn't get a student visa. While my children and I stayed in India, he went back to our home country. Somalia remained an important place for him even though we were separated by distance during those years.

During our difficult years in India, the United Nations provided us with a lot of help. Their support was crucial as we navigated life in a new country with many challenges. Without their assistance, it would have been much harder for my children and me to manage.

In July 2013, my children and I moved to the United States after our time in India ended. This move marked a new beginning for us, and it is why we are here today. Adjusting to life in the U.S. has taken time, but we are slowly settling in and finding stability.

We have been living in St. Paul, Minnesota for one year and five months now. It’s been a big change, but things are getting better. This city is where we are building our new life after moving to the United States.

The 2008 financial crisis had a big impact on us, especially in the United Arab Emirates where we were living at the time. It caused my husband to lose his job in 2009, which forced us to leave the country. That event changed the course of our lives and led us to move to India and eventually the United States.
